title: The e-Business Supply Chain: Meeting Customer Requirements in the Internet Economy
author: Tim A. Minahan
source_file: 2000 Oracle The e-Business Supply Chain_ Meeting Cu...r Requirements in the Internet Economy.pdf
abstract: This Aberdeen White Paper, sponsored by Oracle Corporation, defines the requirements for a comprehensive e-Business supply chain solution in the emerging Internet economy. Aberdeen Group analysis identifies eight criteria for an effective e-Business supply-chain solution — from Internet computing architecture to modular design — and concludes that Oracle is well-positioned to deliver a comprehensive, integrated Internet-based supply-chain suite.
importance_rationale: Published in January 2000 at peak dot-com investment in supply-chain digitization, this vendor-sponsored white paper by Aberdeen's supply-chain analyst Tim Minahan articulated an eight-criteria framework that influenced enterprise evaluation of e-procurement and supply-chain platforms. As a vendor-sponsored document, its independence is limited.
relevance_rationale: The eight-criteria framework for e-Business supply chain (Internet architecture, self-service, demand-driven, common data model, analytics, integration, open platform, modularity) maps closely to modern supply chain platform evaluation criteria. Oracle E-Business Suite remains in active use through 2035.
prescience_rationale: Oracle's prediction of supply-chain internet integration has proven directionally correct — Oracle E-Business Suite has been a leading platform for 25 years (extended support through 2035). However, the dot-com-era framing missed the rise of SaaS/cloud ERP (Salesforce, Workday, SAP S/4HANA) that has disrupted on-premise ERP since 2010.
